name: L'Isle-Jourdain and Cologne Loop in Gascogne Toulousaine
name_original: L'Isle-Jourdain et Cologne – boucle en Gascogne Toulousaine
description: Explore the L'Isle-Jourdain and Cologne cycling loop, a difficult 38.2-mile route through Toulousaine Gascony's historic villages and green
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around L'Isle-Jourdain traverse the rolling countryside of the Save Valley in Gascony. The region features a diverse landscape of agricultural areas, picturesque lakes, and forests, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Routes often include mostly paved surfaces, with some sections offering more challenging segments. The area's elevation is generally moderate, with hills under 200m. Best touring cycling routes around L'Isle-Jourdain The most popular touring cycling route is L'Isle-Jourdain and Lac de Thoux Saint-Cricq Loop, a 24.1 miles (38.7 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route features mostly paved surfaces and is suitable for various skill levels. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is L'Isle-Jourdain and Cologne Loop in Gascogne Toulousaine, a difficult 38.2 miles (61.4 km) path. This route connects L'Isle-Jourdain with Cologne, offering views of the Gascogne Toulousaine. Local touring cyclists also love the L'Isle-Jourdain – Samatan Covered Market loop from L'Isle-Jourdain, a 28.0 miles (45.0 km) trail leading through the rolling countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Touring cycling around L'Isle-Jourdain is defined by rolling hills, the Save Valley, and scenic lakes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging excursions. The routes in L'Isle-Jourdain are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 27 reviews. More than 230 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore L'Isle-Jourdain's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around L'Isle-Jourdain?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes in the L'Isle-Jourdain area, offering a diverse range of options for cyclists. These routes traverse the rolling countryside of the Save Valley, featuring agricultural areas, picturesque lakes, and forests.

Q: What is the general terrain like for touring cycling in L'Isle-Jourdain?
A: The terrain around L'Isle-Jourdain is characterized by rolling countryside and gentle slopes, largely occupied by agriculture. Most routes feature mostly paved surfaces, making for smoother rides. While the elevation is generally moderate with hills under 200m, some routes include unpaved or more challenging segments for those seeking a greater challenge.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in L'Isle-Jourdain?
A: The touring cycling routes around L'Isle-Jourdain are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 27 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, scenic views of the Gascogne Toulousaine, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in L'Isle-Jourdain?
A: Yes, L'Isle-Jourdain offers several touring cycling routes suitable for beginners and families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021124211/l-isle-jourdain-and-lac-de-thoux-saint-cricq-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Isle-Jourdain and Lac de Thoux Saint-Cricq Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route featuring mostly paved surfaces, making it accessible for various skill levels. The region's generally moderate elevation also contributes to a pleasant experience for less experienced riders.

Q: What options are there for more challenging touring cycling routes?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, L'Isle-Jourdain offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021119532/l-isle-jourdain-and-cologne-loop-in-gascogne-toulousaine" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Isle-Jourdain and Cologne Loop in Gascogne Toulousaine&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 61.4 km path with significant elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35826623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Isle-Jourdain – Village d'endoufielle loop from L'Isle-Jourdain&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 76 km with substantial ascent.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the L'Isle-Jourdain area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in L'Isle-Jourdain are designed as scenic loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Popular examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021124211/l-isle-jourdain-and-lac-de-thoux-saint-cricq-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Isle-Jourdain and Lac de Thoux Saint-Cricq Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35826241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Isle-Jourdain – Samatan Covered Market loop from L'Isle-Jourdain&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see along touring cycling routes?
A: Touring cycling routes around L'Isle-Jourdain offer views of the Save Valley, rolling Gascony hillsides, and picturesque lakes. You might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5174963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bouconne Forest&lt;/a&gt;, known as a hiker's paradise, or the recreational &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3124667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordette Lake&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features charming villages and agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots to look out for?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/155744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Castéra&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive vistas of the surrounding countryside. Routes often traverse the Gascogne Toulousaine, offering breathtaking views of the rolling hills and charming villages. The Lac de L'Isle-Jourdain itself is a central hub for recreation and offers pleasant scenery.

Q: What are the parking options for cyclists in L'Isle-Jourdain?
A: L'Isle-Jourdain, being a hub for outdoor activities, likely offers various parking options, especially near its leisure base (Base de Loisirs) and the Lac de L'Isle-Jourdain, which are popular starting points for routes. Specific parking details for individual routes can often be found on their respective komoot tour pages.

Q: Is L'Isle-Jourdain accessible by public transport for cyclists?
A: Yes, L'Isle-Jourdain is accessible by public transport. The town is recognized as a 'Gare Étape' (station stop), indicating its connection to the rail network, which can be convenient for cyclists traveling with their bikes. For more information on rail services, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.visit-occitanie.com/en/rail-tour/gares-etapes/lisle-jourdain/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visit-occitanie.com&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in L'Isle-Jourdain?
A: The Gascony region, where L'Isle-Jourdain is located, generally enjoys pleasant weather for cycling from spring through autumn. Spring offers lush green landscapes, while autumn provides comfortable temperatures and beautiful fall colors. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are often ideal for rides. It's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before planning your trip.

Q: Are there any very long touring cycling routes for multi-day trips?
A: While many routes are designed for day trips, the region also caters to longer adventures. Some routes exceed 100 km, such as the 'L'Isle-Jourdain-foret de Fabas-L'islejourdain' road cycling route. Additionally, the area is part of the historic GR®653, one of the main routes leading to Santiago de Compostela, offering potential for multi-day touring with a blend of heritage and diverse geography.
tips: Small town in Toulouse Gascony where you can discover the European Museum of Campanaire Art under the old grain market, the Claude Augé house, or the Saint-Martin collegiate church.

Cologne in the Gers is not known for its water but for its beautiful medieval square and its beautiful hall.
Cologne was built on the plan of royal lowland bastides: the city stretched around two perpendicular axes. The city is made up of square or perpendicular blocks around the central square and its hall.
The hall surrounds a building with belfry and bell
The square is superb with its cover and its half-timbered houses.
A vestige of moats is also present.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 61 km long and takes an average touring cyclist about 3 hours and 47 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and whether you're using an electric bike.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the towns along the route, such as L'Isle-Jourdain or Cologne. These towns serve as excellent starting points for the loop.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characterized by a hilly landscape with several climbs, some of which can be short and steep. The majority of the route consists of asphalt and paved roads, making it suitable for touring bikes. You'll encounter green hills, vineyards, and valleys throughout the ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The most pleasant times to cycle this route are during spring or autumn. The weather is generally milder, and the Gascon countryside is particularly beautiful with lush greenery or autumnal colors. Summer can be quite hot, especially in the afternoon.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: No, this cycling loop follows public roads and paths, so no permits or entrance fees are required to access or complete the route.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the route primarily uses public roads and paths, dogs are generally allowed, provided they are kept on a leash and you adhere to local regulations. Always ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling alongside you and that you carry water for them.

faqs.question: What cultural and historical points of interest can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This loop is rich in history and culture. You'll pass through towns like L'Isle-Jourdain, known for its medieval heritage, and Cologne, a beautiful bastide town with a well-preserved market hall. The route also follows parts of the historic Saint James Way (GR®653) and passes through Gimont, a bastide town with historical accommodations and churches.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, parts of this cycling route follow sections of the historic Saint James Way (GR®653). Additionally, some sections around L'Isle-Jourdain utilize greenways that were originally developed for A380 transport, offering pleasant car-free cycling paths.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and appealing scenery, including the green, rolling hills of Gascony, picturesque vineyards, and charming, authentic villages. The route offers beautiful views over valleys and provides a deep dive into the intimate countryside of Gascony.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' classification, length of over 60 km, and hilly terrain with some steep sections, this route might be challenging for younger children or families not accustomed to longer, more strenuous rides. It is more suitable for cyclists with good fitness or those using electric bikes.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several charming towns and villages such as L'Isle-Jourdain, Cologne, and Gimont. These locations offer opportunities for refreshments at cafes or restaurants, and some may provide accommodation options, especially in Gimont which has historical accommodations for pilgrims.
